summary: Gruber shares his last-minute predictions for Apple's October 2021 'Unleashed' event, focusing on expected new MacBook Pros with redesigned bodies, new ports, and Apple Silicon chips. He predicts slightly thicker bodies for better cooling, an SD card slot and possibly HDMI, new MagSafe, and expresses hope for a Touch Bar evolution rather than a retreat to F-keys. He emphasizes that sustained GPU performance per watt will be the real story, and expresses wishful thinking about new large iMacs and a prosumer standalone display.
key_points: Predicts MacBook Pros will be slightly thicker to enable better sustained performance through improved cooling, not just peak speed
Hopes Apple evolves the Touch Bar rather than retreating to traditional F-keys, arguing the concept was right but the execution wasn't good enough
Believes GPU performance per watt will be the most shocking revelation, though apps need Metal API adoption to take advantage
Calls out the gap in Apple's display lineup — people want a standalone display that isn't $5,000, and third-party makers won't fill the void
money_quotes: I just can't shake the feeling that the Touch Bar was a wonderful idea but the first crack at it wasn't good enough.
People need to *feel* keys — your eyes are on the display, not the keyboard.
I think a big part of the story will be *sustained* performance, not just peak performance.
We know from the late great iMac Pro that Apple can engineer incredible cooling systems that run nearly silently. Do it for pro MacBooks now.
What I think might prove shocking is the GPU performance of these chips. Particularly performance per watt.
Third-party display makers clearly are not capable of or willing to serve the Mac market.
People don't want to spend $5,000 on a Pro Display XDR.
key_insight: The real story of Apple's pro silicon won't be peak benchmark numbers but sustained performance and GPU efficiency, which will require thicker hardware designs and developer adoption of Metal APIs.
